body:
Title: CRA Form T2203 2025 Yukon, Northwest Territories, and Nunavut multiple-jurisdiction tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2203/t2203-25e.pdf

CRA T2203 2025 Form YT428MJ calculates Yukon tax for multiple jurisdictions. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.064, 0.09, 0.109, 0.4414, and 0.439. YT428MJ tax brackets use thresholds $57,375, $114,750, $177,882, and $500,000; rates 6.4%, 9%, 10.9%, 12.8%, and 15%; and base amounts $0.00, $3,672.00, $8,835.75, $15,717.14, and $56,948.24. Lines 10 to 18 add unallocated tax on split income, subtract non-refundable credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover at 44.14%, and add additional tax for minimum tax purposes at 43.9%. Lines 19 to 30 multiply by the Yukon allocation percentage, apply the resident adjustment based on lines 58189, 58310, 58326, 58330, and 58360 at 6.4%, dividend tax credit, the percentage not allocated to Yukon, and territorial foreign tax credit. Lines 32 to 48 subtract business investment tax credit capped at $25,000, Yukon First Nations income tax credit, and political contribution tax credit, then enter Yukon tax on Part 5 line 10.

CRA T2203 2025 Form NT428MJ calculates Northwest Territories tax for multiple jurisdictions. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.059, 0.086, 0.122, and 0.45. NT428MJ tax brackets use thresholds $51,964, $103,930, and $168,967; rates 5.9%, 8.6%, 12.2%, and 14.05%; and base amounts $0.00, $3,065.88, $7,534.95, and $15,469.47. Lines 10 to 18 add unallocated tax on split income, subtract non-refundable credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover at 45%, and add additional tax for minimum tax purposes at 45%. Lines 19 to 30 multiply by the Northwest Territories allocation percentage, apply the resident adjustment based on line 58360 at 5.9%, dividend tax credit, the percentage not allocated to Northwest Territories, and territorial foreign tax credit. Lines 31 to 33 subtract political contribution tax credit and enter Northwest Territories tax on Part 5 line 11.

CRA T2203 2025 Form NU428MJ calculates Nunavut tax for multiple jurisdictions. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.04, 0.07, 0.09, and 0.45. NU428MJ tax brackets use thresholds $54,707, $109,413, and $177,881; rates 4%, 7%, 9%, and 11.5%; and base amounts $0.00, $2,188.28, $6,017.70, and $12,179.82. Lines 10 to 18 add unallocated tax on split income, subtract non-refundable credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover at 45%, and add additional tax for minimum tax purposes at 45%. Lines 19 to 30 multiply by the Nunavut allocation percentage, apply the resident adjustment based on lines 58230 and 58360 at 4%, dividend tax credit, the percentage not allocated to Nunavut, and territorial foreign tax credit. Lines 31 to 32 subtract the $722 volunteer firefighters and search and rescue volunteers credit and enter Nunavut tax on Part 5 line 12.
